name: Castillo de Valderrobres - return via the Pena reservoir
name_original: Castillo de Valderrobres - vuelta por el pantano de Pena
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Beceite traverse the rugged Puertos de Beceite mountain range, characterized by sharp hills, deep canyons, and river valleys. The region features a network of quiet, well-maintained roads winding through pine forests, oak groves, and agricultural lands with olive and almond trees. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from significant elevation gains in mountainous areas to more gentle stretches along riverbeds. These roads offer diverse scenery for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Beceite The most popular road cycling route is Castillo de Valderrobres - return via the Pena reservoir, a 35.0 miles (56.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete, offering scenic views around Valderrobres and the Pena reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Beceite Village – Natural Pool of El Azud loop from Beceite / Beseit, a moderate 16.9 miles (27.1 km) path. This route explores river valleys and natural pools near Beceite. Local road cyclists also love the Valderrobres Stone Bridge – La Fresneda loop from Valderrobres / Vall de Roures, a 31.2 miles (50.3 km) trail leading through the picturesque Matarraña region, often completed in about 2 hours 25 minutes. Road cycling around Beceite is defined by the mountainous Puertos de Beceite, winding river valleys, and extensive olive and almond groves. The network offers options for both challenging climbs with significant elevation and more relaxed rides through varied terrain. The routes in Beceite are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 370 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Beceite's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Beceite?
A: Road cycling routes around Beceite traverse the rugged Puertos de Beceite mountain range, featuring sharp hills, deep canyons, and winding river valleys. You'll encounter a mix of challenging ascents and exhilarating descents on quiet, well-maintained roads. The scenery varies from lush pine forests and oak groves to extensive olive and almond orchards, offering diverse vistas throughout your ride.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Beceite area?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes available around Beceite on komoot, catering to various skill levels. This includes 3 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 11 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Beceite?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, the region offers sections of the &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde del Val de Zafán&lt;/strong&gt;, an old railway route converted into a greenway. This provides a flatter, more relaxed cycling option, particularly suitable for families or those seeking a gentler ride through picturesque landscapes, tunnels, and bridges. Sections like the one from Cretas to Horta de Sant Joan are ideal for children.

Q: What are some highly-rated road cycling routes in Beceite?
A: The komoot community highly rates routes in Beceite, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Popular choices include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958549311/castillo-de-valderrobres-return-via-the-pena-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Valderrobres - return via the Pena reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, a 56.3 km trail offering scenic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38209771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beceite – La Fresneda loop from la Botera&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 78.3 km path through the picturesque Matarraña region.

Q: What natural attractions can I see near the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can find stunning gorges and waterfalls, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parrissal Walkways&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Estrets Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, which showcase deep canyons carved by rivers. For waterfalls, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portellada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes offer views of these features from nearby roads.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Beceite?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40334371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valderrobres Castle loop from Valderrobres&lt;/a&gt; is a 46.1 km trail that takes you through the historic town and its surrounding landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31712820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beceite – Valderrobres Stone Bridge loop from Beceite / Beseit&lt;/a&gt;, a 54.1 km route offering a scenic circular experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Beceite?
A: Beceite's climate, with its humid and rich environment, supports diverse vegetation, making it enjoyable across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, with lush landscapes and comfortable conditions. Summers can be warm, but early morning rides are often refreshing. Winters are milder, though some higher elevation routes might be cooler.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while cycling in Beceite?
A: The Puertos de Beceite natural park is home to a rich array of wildlife. Cyclists might spot Spanish ibex, golden eagles, griffon vultures, and wild boars, especially in quieter, more remote sections of the routes. The diverse natural environment provides excellent opportunities for nature observation.

Q: Are there services for cyclists in the Matarraña region, including Beceite?
A: Yes, the wider Matarraña region is cycling-friendly and offers dedicated services. These include accommodation tailored to cyclists' needs, bicycle transport, luggage transfer for multi-day tours, repair services, and bike rentals, ensuring a comfortable and convenient cycling experience.

Q: What makes the roads in Beceite suitable for road cycling?
A: The roads in Beceite and the Matarraña region are known for being quiet, with minimal traffic, which significantly enhances safety and enjoyment. They are generally well-maintained, offering good asphalt surfaces. These secondary and regional roads are often sinuous, providing engaging rides through varied terrain and allowing riders to fully immerse themselves in the natural surroundings.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in Beceite?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Beceite offers routes with significant elevation gains. The mountainous surroundings provide climbs that can reach over 1000 meters, such as those found around Monroyo, including ascents like Costa del Ginebre and Torremiró. These routes require good fitness but reward riders with breathtaking views.
tips: Baceite is a Spanish municipality and town in the province of Teruel located in the Matarraña region that offers us a wide variety of routes, natural and cultural heritage, or visits to museums and/or leisure centers. We can find more information at the tourist office or the website https://www.beceite.es/
The Pena reservoir is located within the Matarraña region surrounded by beautiful mountains such as the Sierra de Arcas that make this place a must for nature and sports lovers.

Fuentespalda is located halfway along the road that goes from Vall-de-roures to Pena-roja and Mont-roig de Tastavins and its name would come from focem (de) spathula, the gorge of the small swordfish.
Old stone bridge over the river Matarraña through the town of Valderrobres. It is part of the entrance to the town's old town from a wall where today you can see a group of houses on the riverbed.
The church of Santa María is one of the greatest exponents of Gothic architecture in Aragon and is connected to the Valderrobres castle-palace, which is also Gothic.

waypoints.name: Valderrobres Old Town and Stone Bridge
